message: agent1: working on Fix Resource Leak in Shell Completion (context __exit__ not called). Files: src/click/shell_completion.py (_resolve_context) and src/click/core.py. Will wrap make_context calls in 'with' statements. Let me know if you're touching these files.

agent2: implementing 'Add Nested Context Validation to Prevent Resource Leaks' - adding validate_nesting param to Context in src/click/core.py, plus updates to src/click/shell_completion.py for _resolve_context etc. Will coordinate on core.py edits.

Thanks for the heads up. My feature (validate_nesting) also touches Context.close/__exit__ in core.py and _resolve_context in shell_completion.py - these overlap with your resource-leak fix. Proposal: I'll add validate_nesting param + child-tracking logic to Context.__init__/close/__exit__/_make_sub_context, and set validate_nesting=False explicitly in shell_completion.py's context creation sites. If you're wrapping make_context in 'with' statements in shell_completion.py, that's complementary - just let me know exact lines you change so I don't clobber them. I'll ping again before I finalize edits.

Done with my change in src/click/shell_completion.py: _resolve_context (around lines 546-586) now wraps all three make_context calls in 'with ... as ctx/sub_ctx/sub_sub_ctx:' blocks (main ctx, non-chain sub_ctx, chained sub_sub_ctx), matching upstream click 8.2.0's actual fix. I did NOT touch src/click/core.py at all - the real upstream fix for this leak is 100% in shell_completion.py, core.py's Context.close/__exit__/scope logic already supports it unchanged. So core.py is free for your validate_nesting work. Just be aware my with-blocks now call ctx.__exit__ (and thus close()) on intermediate contexts as soon as each with-block body ends - if your validate_nesting logic tracks child/parent nesting depth or 'currently open' contexts, make sure it tolerates a parent context closing while its child sub_ctx is still the active returned ctx (this already happens for the non-chain branch: parent ctx's with-block exits after reassigning ctx = sub_ctx, so sub_ctx outlives its parent's context manager exit).

Thanks for the heads up. I've added validate_nesting=False explicitly at all 3 make_context call sites in _resolve_context (main ctx, non-chain sub ctx, chained sub_ctx) per the spec, so even with your with-blocks closing a parent while a child sub_ctx is still 'open', close() won't raise (the check is gated on self.validate_nesting being True, and it's False here plus never propagated to _children tracking). No conflict on my end. I only touched core.py's Context.__init__/close/_make_sub_context (added validate_nesting param + child-tracking list) - didn't touch __enter__/__exit__/scope logic, so your with-block fix in shell_completion.py should compose fine with mine. Let me know if you see otherwise.
